leads.issues: The homepage's before/after proof section shows two photos labelled 'BEFORE' in a row and only one labelled 'AFTER,' so a visitor never actually sees a matched pair. Separately, image alt text across the site is just raw camera export file names ('IMG_7018_edited.png,' 'IMG_7013.HEIC,' 'IMG_6958.HEIC'), and the logo's alt text literally reads 'Untitled.' There's also no street address or LocalBusiness schema anywhere on the page, only a passing mention of being based in Bridgewater plus a list of the seven Adelaide Hills suburbs served.
leads.opportunity: Fixing the before/after layout into a real matched pair, replacing the file-name alt text with descriptive keyworded text, and adding full NAP + LocalBusiness schema for Bridgewater and the Hills suburbs are all same-week fixes. Expected within 30 days: better matching for 'chimney sweep Adelaide Hills' and 'chimney sweep Bridgewater' searches, plus a before/after section that actually builds trust with someone about to let a stranger into their roof space.
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I was looking at chimney sweeps around the Adelaide Hills and landed on Hills Chimney Sweep's site. A couple things jumped out - your before/after section on the homepage has two 'BEFORE' photos and only one 'AFTER,' so it's hard to actually see the transformation you're pitching. I also noticed the photo alt text across the site is just the raw camera filenames (things like 'IMG_7018_edited.png' and 'IMG_6958.HEIC') instead of real descriptions, and your logo's alt text is literally 'Untitled' - that's invisible to Google Image Search and screen readers.

I put together a page showing the fix and what it'd look like:
https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-16-hills-chimney-sweep.html

You can also see how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all, just thought it was worth flagging for a business covering as much of the Hills as you do.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.industry: Dance Studio (Ballet, Jazz, Hip Hop, Acro)
leads.issues: The homepage's footer 'email us' link is coded as mailto:905-727-3535/dynamicdancecompanyinc@gmail.com - the phone number is jammed directly into the email address, so most phones and browsers can't open a valid compose window from it. The hero copy also repeats 'Welcome to Dynamic Dance Company Inc.' three times in a row as one run-on headline, and the entire welcome block (headline, 'Top choice dance school 2026' line, and CTA) appears twice down the page. On top of that, the homepage's meta description - the exact text Google shows in search results - contains a typo: 'offering recreational and competitive dances classes for ages 3 and up' instead of 'dance classes.'
leads.opportunity: Fixing the broken mailto link, removing the duplicated welcome block, and correcting the meta description typo are all same-week fixes. Expected within 30 days: parents who try to email from the footer actually reach the studio instead of hitting a dead link, and a homepage that leads with the real 'Top Choice Dance School 2026' win instead of repeated placeholder-style copy.
leads.email_subject: Your footer email link is actually broken
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I was checking out dance studios around Aurora and came across Dynamic Dance Company - congrats on the Top Choice 2026 badge on your homepage. Two things I noticed while I was there: the footer's 'email us' link is actually broken - it's coded as mailto:905-727-3535/dynamicdancecompanyinc@gmail.com, so your phone number gets jammed into the email address and most phones/browsers can't open a compose window from it. Separately, the meta description Google shows for your homepage has a small typo - 'offering recreational and competitive dances classes for ages 3 and up' instead of 'dance classes.'

I put together a page showing both fixes:
https://organic-theory.vercel.app/leads/funnels/2026-07-16-dynamic-dance-company.html

You can also see how I work at https://organic-theory.vercel.app

No pressure at all - just didn't want a parent trying to email you from the footer to hit a dead end.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

The homepage also still has a 'Contactless Services' section reading 'We are experiencing the NEW NORMAL. After the events of the last few weeks...' - leftover COVID-2020 copy still live on the site. On top of that, the homepage's hero slider text renders broken, repeating 'Wendy & Tom [BOOK AN APPOINTMENT] Wendy [BOOK AN APPOINTMENT] & Tom' several times instead of a clean headline.
leads.opportunity: Fixing the mismatched header mailto link, retiring the 2020 COVID copy, and fixing the broken slider text are all same-week fixes. leads.email_subject: Two different emails on your homepage
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I was looking at alterations specialists in Newmarket and found Wendy & Tom's site - working with names like Trelise Cooper and Astra Bridal is a great trust signal to have upfront. One thing I noticed: the email link in your header shows 'wendytomworkshop@gmail.com' but it's actually coded to open a compose window to a different address, 'info@wendytomworkshop.co.nz' - so the two don't match, which could send a bride's message somewhere you're not expecting. I also noticed the homepage still has a 'Contactless Services' section talking about 'the new normal' from the pandemic, which reads a bit dated in 2026.

Gabriel Balenton | Organic Theory Founder

leads.issues: Checking Google's own index of the site, six separate pages - the homepage, Contact, About, About Craig, Repairs, and shop-online-accessories - all carry the exact same ~160-character title tag verbatim: 'Naples and Collier County's Finest Music Store for Musical Instrument Repairs, New & Used Band Instruments, Rentals, Accessories, Lessons, and Print Music.' That's roughly 2.5x longer than the ~60 characters Google actually displays, so it's truncated mid-sentence in every search result no matter which page ranks, and none of Craig's real credentials (University of Miami/FIU repair work, Naples Philharmonic soloist) ever make it into a title searchers can see.
leads.opportunity: Splitting that one repeated string into six page-specific, under-60-character titles is a same-day fix. Expected within 30 days: search results that actually differ by page, full (non-truncated) snippets, and titles that can finally surface the University of Miami/FIU and Naples Philharmonic credentials that set Craig apart from big-box music stores.

leads.issues: Google's index shows two separate, live contact pages for the same business: the old, non-www static hillsantiqueclockrepair.com/contactus.html and the newer hillsantiqueclockrepair.com/index.php/contact-2/. The same pattern repeats elsewhere - old static pages (Recentrestoration.html, Tallclocks.html) sit alongside a completely separate index.php/repairs/ and index.php/about-us/ structure, meaning two different site architectures from a past migration are both still crawlable and indexed at once.
leads.opportunity: Picking one canonical URL structure and 301-redirecting the leftover static .html pages into it is roughly a one-week fix. Expected within 30 days: search authority that's no longer split between duplicate pages, a cleaner crawl for Google, and visitors landing on one consistent page instead of whichever old or new version happens to be linked.
leads.email_subject: Google has two different "contact" pages for your shop
leads.email_body:
Hi,

I was looking at antique clock restorers in the MetroWest area and noticed something on Hills Antique Clocks' site - Google's index shows two separate, live contact pages: the old contactus.html and a newer index.php/contact-2/. The same thing shows up elsewhere too - old static pages like Tallclocks.html and Recentrestoration.html sit next to a completely different index.php-based structure, which looks like a leftover from a past site migration that never got fully cleaned up.
